title: Epistemology
description: How we know - knowledge confidence and provenance

canonical-dsl-pattern.name: Canonical DSL Preservation
canonical-dsl-pattern.description: NEVER translate MoneyWorks terminology - preserve exact field names to maintain semantic integrity
canonical-dsl-pattern.rationale: MoneyWorks uses GST for ALL tax types globally. Preserving exact terminology prevents semantic drift and maintains API consistency.

field-discovery-pattern.name: XML-based Field Discovery
field-discovery-pattern.description: Since TSV exports lack headers, use XML exports to discover field names and structure
field-discovery-pattern.rationale: MoneyWorks TSV format has no header row, making XML the only way to discover field names and order dynamically.

client-caching-pattern.name: LRU Client Caching
client-caching-pattern.description: Cache MoneyWorks clients with TTL-based cleanup to avoid recreation overhead

multi-format-export-pattern.name: Multiple Export Formats
multi-format-export-pattern.description: Support 4 export formats for different use cases: compact (arrays), compact-headers, full (objects), schema (enriched)

formats.schema: Objects with field metadata for LLM understanding

middleware-pipeline-pattern.name: Elysia Middleware Chain
middleware-pipeline-pattern.description: Ordered middleware chain: requestId → errorHandler → logging → i18n → auth

branded-types-pattern.name: Branded Type Safety
branded-types-pattern.description: Use branded types for domain primitives to prevent misuse (YYYYMMDD, AccountCode, etc.)
branded-types-pattern.benefit: Compile-time prevention of passing wrong string types (e.g., can't pass ISO date where YYYYMMDD expected)

table-registry-registration-pattern.name: TableRegistry Registration Pattern
table-registry-registration-pattern.description: API controllers MUST be registered in TableRegistry.register() to be available via REST API - registration is NOT optional
table-registry-registration-pattern.criticalImportance: HIGH - determines whether entity is 'available' or stuck in 'upcoming' status
examples: ProductController NOT registered (US-001) → Product still in 'upcoming' list
evidence: US-002: Account in 'available' array after registration
US-001: Product in 'upcoming' array without registration
table-registry-registration-pattern.antipattern: Creating controller without registering it - makes entity inaccessible via API

5-file-canonical-pattern.name: 5-File Canonical Entity Structure
5-file-canonical-pattern.description: Canonical entities follow enums → types → fields → validators → index structure for consistency

structure.types.ts: TypeScript interfaces with exact MW field names
structure.fields.ts: Field metadata (dataType, maxLength, description, validation rules)

5-file-canonical-pattern.rationale: Consistent structure improves discoverability and reduces cognitive load when working across entities

repository-mock-mode-pattern.name: Repository Mock Mode Pattern
repository-mock-mode-pattern.description: Repositories support MONEYWORKS_MOCK_MODE env variable with realistic sample data for autonomous testing
repository-mock-mode-pattern.rationale: Enables autonomous testing without live MoneyWorks connection
evidence: US-002: 37 tests passing with MONEYWORKS_MOCK_MODE=true

specialized-repository-methods-pattern.name: Specialized Repository Query Methods
specialized-repository-methods-pattern.description: Repositories provide domain-specific query methods beyond generic CRUD for better DX
specialized-repository-methods-pattern.rationale: Domain-specific methods improve code readability and reduce cognitive load compared to generic filters
evidence: US-002: AccountRepository has 11 specialized query methods

9-step-entity-implementation-pattern.name: 9-Step Entity Implementation Checklist
9-step-entity-implementation-pattern.description: Complete entity implementation requires 9 files across 3 packages: canonical (3), data (3), api (2), plus registry update
9-step-entity-implementation-pattern.rationale: Ensures complete backend entity implementation with no missed exports or registrations
evidence: ACCT-001: All 9 steps followed, Account available in API

triple-export-registration-pattern.name: Triple Export Registration for Repositories
triple-export-registration-pattern.description: New repositories require export in 3 locations: repositories/index.ts, main index.ts, AND both factory functions
triple-export-registration-pattern.antipattern: Forgetting any of the 3 locations causes 'Module has no exported member' errors
evidence: ACCT-001: Initial typecheck failed until all 3 locations updated

repository-query-categories-pattern.name: Repository Query Method Categories
repository-query-categories-pattern.description: Organize repository queries into 5 categories for consistent API design
repository-query-categories-pattern.rationale: Taxonomy helps ensure consistent naming and comprehensive coverage across entities
evidence: ACCT-001: 8 methods organized across categories

bitwise-role-search-pattern.name: MoneyWorks Bitwise Role Search Syntax
bitwise-role-search-pattern.description: MoneyWorks search supports bitwise operations using (Field&#xx)!=0 syntax for efficient role-based filtering
bitwise-role-search-pattern.syntax: (Role&#xx)!=0 where xx is hex role bit
bitwise-role-search-pattern.rationale: Enables server-side filtering without fetching all records, crucial for performance with large contact lists
evidence: CONT-001 L-002: Discovered bitwise search during Contact role implementation

sequence-number-vs-code-primary-key-patt.name: SequenceNumber vs Code Primary Key Strategy
sequence-number-vs-code-primary-key-patt.description: MoneyWorks tables use different primary key strategies: user-defined Code (Products) vs auto-generated SequenceNumber (Names, Contacts)
implications: Repository primaryKey must match table strategy
findByCode() only works for code-based tables
SequenceNumber tables require findBySequenceNumber() or filter queries
sequence-number-vs-code-primary-key-patt.rationale: Table key strategy affects repository design and query patterns
evidence: CONT-001 D-001: Contact uses SequenceNumber not Code

dual-entity-parent-child-pattern.name: Dual-Entity Parent-Child FK Pattern
dual-entity-parent-child-pattern.description: Some MoneyWorks entities require two coordinated types with parent-child FK relationship (e.g., Transaction header + Detail line items)

structure.child: Detail - line items with account/product entries

dual-entity-parent-child-pattern.rationale: Accounting transactions inherently have header/line structure - represents one logical unit but two MW tables
evidence: TXNS-001: First dual-entity implementation with Transaction + Detail

tsv-header-row-import-pattern.name: TSV Header Row for Import
tsv-header-row-import-pattern.description: MoneyWorks TSV import requires field names as first row. Unlike export (no headers), import needs headers for column mapping.
tsv-header-row-import-pattern.implementation: recordsToOrderedTSV() generates header row from discovered schema fields before data rows
tsv-header-row-import-pattern.rationale: MoneyWorks uses header row to match incoming columns to table fields during import
evidence: IMPT-001 L-001: TSV import requires header row with field names

schema-driven-import-pattern.name: Schema-Driven Import Field Ordering
schema-driven-import-pattern.description: Use discovered table schema to determine field order when generating TSV for import. Field order in TSV must match schema.
implementation: 2. Extract fields from schema in correct order
3. Generate TSV with columns matching field order
schema-driven-import-pattern.rationale: JSON doesn't preserve order, but TSV column positions must match MoneyWorks field structure for correct import
evidence: IMPT-001 L-002: Field order matters - schema discovery ensures correct ordering

layered-validation-pattern.name: Layered Pre-Import Validation
layered-validation-pattern.description: Validate records client-side before sending to MoneyWorks to catch errors earlier with better messages
benefits: Better error messages with field-level detail
Reduced round trips to MoneyWorks server
layered-validation-pattern.rationale: MoneyWorks error responses can be cryptic - client-side validation provides clearer feedback
evidence: IMPT-001 L-003: Validation before send saves round trips and improves error messages

react-type-conflicts-pattern.name: Monorepo React Version Type Conflicts
react-type-conflicts-pattern.description: Monorepos with mixed React 18/19 versions cause type conflicts with UI libraries like Radix UI that have strict React type dependencies
symptoms: Radix UI components show type errors despite correct code
react-type-conflicts-pattern.workaround: Use native HTML elements instead of Radix primitives when type conflicts occur
react-type-conflicts-pattern.rootCause: Root package.json has React 18 types, but new packages use React 19
react-type-conflicts-pattern.permanentFix: Standardize React version across all workspace packages
evidence: AIPOC-001 D-001: Native HTML chosen over Radix due to type conflicts

react-router-7-explicit-routing-pattern.name: React Router 7 Explicit Route Configuration
react-router-7-explicit-routing-pattern.description: React Router v7 requires explicit route configuration in app/routes.ts using index() and route() helpers with 'satisfies RouteConfig' pattern
react-router-7-explicit-routing-pattern.rationale: No automatic file-based routing in v7 - routes must be explicitly registered
evidence: AIPOC-001 AC-002: Verified explicit route configuration in app/routes.ts

anthropic-server-streaming-pattern.name: Anthropic SDK Server-Side Streaming
anthropic-server-streaming-pattern.description: Use @anthropic-ai/sdk with messages.stream() and async iteration for server-side AI responses in React Router actions
anthropic-server-streaming-pattern.rationale: Server-side streaming keeps API keys secure and enables SSR-friendly AI integration
evidence: AIPOC-001 AC-007: Server action uses anthropic.messages.stream() with for await loop

tailwind-v4-vite-plugin-pattern.name: Tailwind CSS v4 Vite Plugin
tailwind-v4-vite-plugin-pattern.description: Tailwind CSS v4 uses @tailwindcss/vite plugin directly - no PostCSS configuration required
tailwind-v4-vite-plugin-pattern.rationale: v4 simplifies setup by eliminating PostCSS config - plugin handles everything
evidence: AIPOC-001 AC-003: Tailwind integrated with @tailwindcss/vite plugin

betatool-json-schema-pattern.name: betaTool with JSON Schema for Anthropic SDK
betatool-json-schema-pattern.description: Use betaTool from @anthropic-ai/sdk/helpers/beta/json-schema instead of betaZodTool for React Router 7 / Vite projects to avoid Zod ESM compatibility issues

implementation.pattern: betaTool({ name, description, inputSchema: { type: 'object', properties: {...} }, run: async (input) => {...} })

betatool-json-schema-pattern.rationale: betaZodTool requires Zod's toJSONSchema which has ESM compatibility issues in Vite/React Router environments
evidence: AITOOLS-001: All 6 tools use betaTool successfully
betaZodTool caused import errors in Vite dev server

anthropic-toolrunner-pattern.name: Anthropic toolRunner for Automatic Tool Execution
anthropic-toolrunner-pattern.description: Use anthropic.beta.messages.toolRunner() for automatic tool execution loops - handles tool_use stop reason and re-submission automatically

implementation.usage: const response = await anthropic.beta.messages.toolRunner({ model, max_tokens, tools: [tool1, tool2], messages })

anthropic-toolrunner-pattern.rationale: toolRunner abstracts the tool execution loop - automatically calls tools and re-submits results until Claude responds without tool_use
evidence: AITOOLS-001 AC-003: Server action uses toolRunner for automatic tool execution loop

tools-vs-skills-distinction.name: Anthropic Tools vs Skills Architecture
tools-vs-skills-distinction.description: Tools execute server-side with full system access (files, bash, network). Skills run in Anthropic's sandboxed container for document generation (XLSX, PDF) with NO network access.

tools.bestFor: File operations, bash, external API calls

tools-vs-skills-distinction.rationale: Use tools for interactive system access, skills for polished document generation
evidence: Research: Skills API docs show no network access
User clarified skills can do code execution but in sandbox

artifact-code-block-pattern.name: AI Artifact Code Block Transport
artifact-code-block-pattern.description: AI returns structured artifact data in fenced ```artifacts code blocks, parsed by frontend regex. Clean separation of conversational text from structured data.

implementation.parsing: parseArtifacts() extracts JSON from code block via regex
implementation.separation: Chat text flows normally, artifacts extracted separately

artifact-code-block-pattern.rationale: Code blocks are easy to parse and maintain clean separation from conversational content
evidence: ARTF-001 D-001: Chose code block format for artifact data transport

discriminated-union-rendering-pattern.name: Discriminated Union Type-Safe Rendering
discriminated-union-rendering-pattern.description: Use TypeScript discriminated unions with type guards for type-safe component selection when rendering multiple artifact types
discriminated-union-rendering-pattern.rationale: Compile-time safety ensures correct component receives correct data shape
evidence: ARTF-001: 9 artifact types with type-safe rendering via discriminated unions

split-panel-responsive-pattern.name: Split Panel Responsive Layout
split-panel-responsive-pattern.description: Desktop split-panel (60/40) with mobile drawer overlay pattern for chat + artifact display

implementation.desktop: flex with w-[60%] chat, w-[40%] artifacts
implementation.mobile: Full-width chat with fixed bottom button to open drawer

split-panel-responsive-pattern.rationale: Split panel works well on wide screens, drawer overlay preserves chat real estate on mobile
evidence: ARTF-001 AC-001, AC-003: Split panel desktop, drawer mobile

artifact-implementation-5-step-pattern.name: 5-Step Artifact Implementation Pattern
artifact-implementation-5-step-pattern.description: Standard pattern for adding new AI artifacts: (1) Types + type guards in types.ts, (2) React component, (3) Renderer integration with discriminated union, (4) Tool enhancement with report definition/generation, (5) System prompt documentation
steps: 1. Add artifact type to ArtifactType union and create data interface with type guard in types.ts
2. Create React component for visual rendering in components/artifacts/{name}.tsx
3. Add discriminated union case in artifact-renderer.tsx with type guard check
4. Add report definition to mw-report tool and implement generation logic
5. Document artifact structure and usage in system prompt (anthropic.server.ts)
artifact-implementation-5-step-pattern.rationale: Proven pattern enables consistent artifact implementation with all necessary integration points
evidence: DTXN-001: Completed in < 1 day following this pattern
References bank-reconciliation-status as prior example
APAY-001: Aged payables with expandable rows confirmed pattern

aging-report-progressive-urgency-pattern.name: Progressive Urgency Visual Indicators for Aging Reports
aging-report-progressive-urgency-pattern.description: Use red (critical) for 3+ months overdue, amber (warning) for 2 months overdue in aging reports. Apply to row backgrounds, text color, and status icons.
aging-report-progressive-urgency-pattern.rationale: Progressive urgency helps users quickly identify items requiring immediate attention while not alarming them about normal items
evidence: APAY-001 AC-006: Visual indicators implemented for overdue amounts
stocktake-report.tsx: Same pattern for low stock alerts

nested-tbody-expandable-rows-pattern.name: Nested tbody for Expandable Table Rows
nested-tbody-expandable-rows-pattern.description: Use nested tbody elements to group parent-child rows in expandable tables. Each expandable section gets its own tbody, allowing proper table structure while enabling independent expansion.
nested-tbody-expandable-rows-pattern.rationale: Maintains proper HTML table semantics while allowing independent grouping of supplier/invoice rows
evidence: APAY-001 D-002: Chose nested tbody over conditional row rendering

us-002-table-registry-validation.description: Verified Account appears in 'available' list after TableRegistry registration
evidence: API response: {available: ['TaxRate', 'Name', 'Account']}
AccountController registered at line 35 of table-registry.ts
Account removed from 'upcoming' array
